heading.en: Things are nouns — add -s for plural

body.en: Name things with nouns. Put a or the before them. Make most plurals by adding -s (dog -> dogs). With 600 thing words plus a/the and -s, you can name the world.
body.zh: 用名词指称事物，前面加 a 或 the。大多数复数加 -s（dog→dogs）。用 600 个“事物”词，加上 a/the 和 -s，就能命名整个世界。

gloss.en: a money record you keep at a bank

words.example: I don't have a bank account.

gloss.en: a single thing that someone does

words.example: That was a kind act.

gloss.en: a thing added to something else

words.example: The new room is a nice addition.

gloss.en: a small change to make something better

words.example: I made a small adjustment to the plan.

gloss.en: a notice that tells people about a product

words.example: I saw the advertisement in the newspaper.

gloss.en: a promise or deal between people

words.example: They made an agreement to work together.

gloss.en: the gas we breathe around us

words.example: Let's go out for some fresh air.

gloss.en: how much there is of something

words.example: He ate a large amount of food.

gloss.en: the feeling of finding something funny

words.example: She smiled with amusement.

gloss.en: a living thing that is not a plant

words.example: A dog is an animal.

gloss.en: what you say back to a question

words.example: I don't know the answer.

gloss.en: the tools needed for a task

words.example: We used special apparatus in the lab.

gloss.en: a sign that you think something is good

words.example: She wanted her father's approval.

gloss.en: a talk where people do not agree

words.example: They had an argument about money.

gloss.en: making beautiful things like paintings

words.example: I like modern art.

gloss.en: a try to hurt someone with violence

words.example: The attack happened last night.

gloss.en: a try to do something hard

words.example: I passed on my first attempt.

gloss.en: looking and listening to something closely

words.example: Please give me your attention.

gloss.en: a fun place to visit

words.example: The palace is a big tourist attraction.

gloss.en: the power to tell people what to do

words.example: She is in a position of authority.

gloss.en: the part behind or at the rear

words.example: We sat in the back of the car.

gloss.en: when things are in equal, good amounts

words.example: It is good to keep a balance in life.

gloss.en: the bottom part something stands on

words.example: The lamp has a heavy base.

gloss.en: the way someone acts

words.example: His behaviour at school is good.

gloss.en: a strong feeling that something is true

words.example: She has a strong belief in God.

gloss.en: the time when a baby is born

words.example: The baby weighed three kilos at birth.

gloss.en: an act of cutting with your teeth

words.example: The dog gave me a small bite.

gloss.en: the red liquid in your body

words.example: He lost a lot of blood.

gloss.en: a hard hit

words.example: She got a blow on the head.

gloss.en: all the parts of a person or animal

words.example: The human body is amazing.

gloss.en: a hard yellow metal

words.example: The door handle is made of brass.

gloss.en: a food made from flour and baked

words.example: I ate a slice of bread.

gloss.en: the air you take into your body

words.example: Take a deep breath.

gloss.en: a boy with the same parents as you

words.example: My brother is older than me.

gloss.en: a place with walls and a roof

words.example: That is a very tall building.

gloss.en: a mark or hurt from fire or heat

words.example: She has a burn on her hand.

gloss.en: to break open suddenly

words.example: The balloon will burst soon.

gloss.en: buying and selling things for money

words.example: He runs a small business.

gloss.en: a soft yellow food made from cream

words.example: I put butter on my bread.

gloss.en: strong cloth used for painting or tents

words.example: The artist painted on a large canvas.

gloss.en: looking after someone's needs

words.example: The sick man needed medical care.

gloss.en: the thing that makes something happen

words.example: What was the cause of the fire?

gloss.en: a soft white stick used to write

words.example: She wrote on the board with chalk.

gloss.en: a possibility that something may happen

words.example: There is a small chance of rain.

gloss.en: when something becomes different

words.example: There is a change in the weather.

gloss.en: material made from cotton, wool, etc.

words.example: She cleaned the table with a cloth.

gloss.en: a hard black rock that is burned for heat

words.example: I put more coal on the fire.

gloss.en: red, blue, green and other such kinds

words.example: What is your favourite colour?

gloss.en: feeling relaxed and free from pain

words.example: This chair gives great comfort.

gloss.en: a group chosen to make decisions

words.example: The committee met to talk about the plan.

gloss.en: a business that sells goods or services

words.example: She works for a big company.

gloss.en: looking at how things are alike or different

words.example: Let's make a comparison of the two cars.

gloss.en: an event where people try to win

words.example: She won the singing competition.

gloss.en: the state that something is in

words.example: The old car is in good condition.

gloss.en: a link between two things

words.example: There is a connection between the two events.

gloss.en: the power to make something do as you want

words.example: She has control of the team.

gloss.en: a person who makes food

words.example: My mother is a very good cook.

gloss.en: a soft red-brown metal

words.example: The wires are made of copper.

gloss.en: a thing made to be the same as another

words.example: Please make a copy of this page.

gloss.en: a light material used to close bottles

words.example: He pulled the cork from the bottle.

gloss.en: a soft white material used to make cloth

words.example: This shirt is made of cotton.

gloss.en: a loud sound when air leaves your throat

words.example: She has a bad cough today.

gloss.en: a land with its own government

words.example: France is a beautiful country.

gloss.en: a thing put over another to protect it

words.example: Put a cover on the book.

gloss.en: a thin line where something is broken

words.example: This cup has a crack in it.

gloss.en: a way to buy now and pay later

words.example: She bought the sofa on credit.

gloss.en: an act that breaks the law

words.example: Stealing is a crime.

gloss.en: to press hard so something breaks

words.example: Be careful not to crush the box.

gloss.en: a loud sound that shows strong feeling

words.example: We heard a cry for help.

gloss.en: a flow of water, air or electricity

words.example: The current in the river is strong.

gloss.en: a line that bends in a smooth way

words.example: The road has a sharp curve.

gloss.en: harm that makes something worse

words.example: The storm caused a lot of damage.

gloss.en: the chance that something bad may happen

words.example: There is danger near the fire.

gloss.en: a person's female child

words.example: They have a son and a daughter.

gloss.en: a time of 24 hours

words.example: What day is it today?

gloss.en: the end of life

words.example: We were sad about his death.

gloss.en: money that you owe to someone

words.example: He needs to pay his debt.

gloss.en: a choice you make after thinking

words.example: I made the right decision.

gloss.en: a unit for measuring temperature

words.example: Water freezes at zero degrees.

gloss.en: the way the parts of something are made

words.example: I like the design of this car.

gloss.en: a strong wish to have something

words.example: He had a strong desire to win.

gloss.en: the act of destroying something

words.example: The war caused great destruction.

gloss.en: a small fact or part of something

words.example: She told me every detail of the story.

gloss.en: the slow growth of something

words.example: We watched the baby's development.

gloss.en: the way your body breaks down food

words.example: Good food helps digestion.

gloss.en: the way a person or thing moves

words.example: They walked in the direction of the village.

gloss.en: finding something new

words.example: They made an important discovery.

gloss.en: a talk about something

words.example: We had a long discussion about the plan.

gloss.en: an illness in people, animals or plants

words.example: Heart disease is very serious.

gloss.en: a strong bad feeling about something

words.example: She looked at the dirty room with disgust.

gloss.en: the space between two places

words.example: We walked a long distance.

gloss.en: the way something is spread over an area

words.example: They studied the distribution of the plants.

gloss.en: splitting something into separate parts

words.example: The cell grows by division.

gloss.en: a feeling of not being sure

words.example: I have no doubt about it.

gloss.en: a liquid you take into your body

words.example: Can I have a drink?

gloss.en: the act of controlling a car

words.example: Driving in the city is hard.

gloss.en: fine dry powder of earth or dirt

words.example: There is dust on the table.

gloss.en: the world we live on

words.example: We live on the earth.

gloss.en: the outside line or part of something

words.example: He stood at the edge of the cliff.
